consistency.name: Consistent with typical D&D 5e spell naming conventions.
consistency.level: Level 2 is a standard spell level.
consistency.school: Abjuration is appropriate for a protective/buffing spell like this.
consistency.range: Touch is standard for targeted buffs.
consistency.duration: 1 hour is a common duration for mid-combat or exploration buffs.
consistency.classes: Cleric is a primary caster for this type of protective magic.

quality.completeness: All required fields are present and well-populated.
quality.clarity: The description is very clear regarding the dual effects (AC/Save buff + Damage sharing) and termination conditions.
quality.component_detail: The material components are specific and include a financial value and usage condition (must be worn), which is high quality.
quality.mechanics_clarity: The core mechanic (sharing damage) is explicitly stated and balanced by the powerful defensive buff (+1 to AC/Saves and resistance to all damage).

mechanics.cost_and_risk: The primary risk is that the caster takes all damage the target receives. This represents a significant risk/reward trade-off for the powerful defense granted to the target.
limitations: Range limit of 60 feet between caster and target.
Ends if caster drops to 0 HP.
Requires specific, persistent material components (platinum rings).
mechanics.balance_assessment: This is a very potent Level 2 spell. Resistance to *all* damage is extremely powerful for a single target. The trade-off (caster takes all damage) effectively forces the caster to remain near the target, or risk their own immediate incapacitation. The 60ft range limit is crucial for maintaining balance.

analysis.suggested_actions: No errors found. The data is high quality and mechanically sound according to established system rules.
